response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software craftsman with 15+ years spanning web, mobile, and application-layer development, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator development, GPU architecture analysis, or deep-learning workload optimization. His most technically adjacent work — low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby and C/C++ components at National Instruments — operates entirely at the software layer and does not approach the hardware performance modeling or silicon architecture feedback the role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse engineering experience demonstrating strong software fundamentals and adaptability across stacks
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work using async I/O and performance-sensitive architectures at Dunnhumby
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic tooling at re:cinq shows some awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
No background in deep-learning workload analysis, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or AI accelerator performance optimization
Educational background (BS CS + BA Fine Arts) and career trajectory are firmly application-software-focused, with no signal of a pivot toward hardware or ML systems

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware background
No deep learning or AI accelerator domain expertise
